Core Method.label: Core Method
Core Method.description: Theories, indicators, and analytical methods that structure the case-study model.

Dataset / Data Source.label: Dataset / Data Source
Dataset / Data Source.description: Primary datasets, administrative sources, and processed data artifacts used by the analysis.

Software / Tool.label: Software / Tool
Software / Tool.description: Libraries, routing engines, and reproducible code tools used to implement the workflow.

Validation / Benchmark.label: Validation / Benchmark
Validation / Benchmark.description: Benchmarks, sensitivity checks, robustness tests, and limitations used to evaluate results.

Policy / Planning Context.label: Policy / Planning Context
Policy / Planning Context.description: Planning, equity, logistics, or policy sources that frame the implication of the findings.

Background / Interpretation.label: Background / Interpretation
Background / Interpretation.description: Contextual sources and cautious interpretation notes that shape how the result should be read.

transit.title: Transit Accessibility in DKI Jakarta
transit.summary: This visual map summarizes how key references, datasets, tools, and methods support the analysis. The full reference list is preserved below.

center.label: Transit accessibility and spatial equity in DKI Jakarta
center.title: How evenly can residents reach essential facilities by transit within 60 minutes, and what does Mikrotrans add?

steps.title: Research question
steps.body: Start from the case-study question at the center of the graph.

steps.title: Core methods
steps.body: Reveal the methods and theory that define the analytical structure.

steps.title: Data foundations
steps.body: Add the datasets and source material used to operationalize the model.

steps.title: Implementation tools
steps.body: Show the software and technical workflow used to build the analysis.

steps.title: Validation and context
steps.body: Bring in validation, policy context, and interpretation references.

steps.title: Connections
steps.body: Draw the relationship labels that connect each source to the analysis.

steps.title: Explore
steps.body: Select or focus a node to inspect its role and connected evidence.

nodes.label: Jaklingko GTFS scenarios A and B
nodes.title: Jaklingko GTFS scenarios A and B
nodes.role: Defines the backbone-only and backbone-plus-Mikrotrans transit networks used in the accessibility comparison.

nodes.label: RW centroids, kelurahan boundaries and RDTR overlay
nodes.title: RW centroids, kelurahan boundaries and RDTR overlay
nodes.role: Provides the origin geography, aggregation units, and planning-zoning context for interpreting accessibility.

nodes.label: 27 essential-facility destination categories
nodes.title: 27 essential-facility destination categories
nodes.role: Supplies the reachable opportunities counted in the AHP-weighted cumulative opportunities indicator.

nodes.label: Accessibility evaluation of land-use and transport strategies
nodes.title: Accessibility evaluation of land-use and transport strategies
nodes.role: Provides the place-based accessibility framework used to connect land use, transport, and temporal service conditions.

nodes.label: How accessibility shapes land use
nodes.title: How accessibility shapes land use
nodes.role: Foundational accessibility concept supporting the cumulative-opportunities framing.

nodes.label: Measuring accessibility: positive and normative implementations
nodes.title: Measuring accessibility: positive and normative implementations
nodes.role: Helps position cumulative opportunities among alternative accessibility indicator families.

nodes.label: Analytic Hierarchy Process
nodes.title: Analytic Hierarchy Process references
nodes.role: Supports the pairwise weighting structure used to prioritize essential facility categories.

nodes.label: R5 / r5r multimodal routing
nodes.title: R5 / r5r multimodal routing reference
nodes.role: Supports the R5-based transit travel-time matrix logic behind the r5py routing workflow.

nodes.label: Getis-Ord Gi* spatial association statistic
nodes.title: Getis-Ord Gi* spatial association statistic
nodes.role: Provides the spatial cluster test used to identify accessibility hot spots and cold spots.

nodes.label: Uncertainty and variation in public transport accessibility metrics
nodes.title: Uncertainty and variation in public transport accessibility metrics
nodes.role: Supports the treatment of departure-window variation and uncertainty in transit accessibility metrics.

nodes.label: Continuous accessibility and transit commute mode share
nodes.title: Continuous accessibility and transit commute mode share
nodes.role: Supports the reading of transit accessibility over a departure window instead of a single lucky departure.

nodes.label: Transit accessibility, equity and spatial structure
nodes.title: Transit accessibility, equity and spatial structure references
nodes.role: Cervero, El-Geneidy, Lucas, Pereira, and Sharma frame the results as spatial equity and planning evidence.

nodes.label: Center-periphery gap and Mikrotrans uplift
nodes.title: Center-periphery gap and Mikrotrans uplift
nodes.role: Links the evidence base to the reported 34x inequality ratio, +26.6% mean uplift, and remaining peripheral gaps.

pangan.title: Critical Roads for the Rice Supply Chain in West Java
pangan.summary: This visual map connects the supply-chain data, spatial-interaction model, software workflow, validation checks, and planning interpretation. The full reference list is preserved below.

center.label: Critical road segments for West Java rice supply chain
center.title: Which road segments are most critical for rice movement from production centers through channels to markets?

steps.title: Research question
steps.body: Start from the case-study question at the center of the graph.

steps.title: Core methods
steps.body: Reveal the methods and theory that define the analytical structure.

steps.title: Data foundations
steps.body: Add the datasets and source material used to operationalize the model.

steps.title: Implementation tools
steps.body: Show the software and technical workflow used to build the analysis.

steps.body: Bring in validation, policy context, and interpretation references.

nodes.label: BPS Jawa Barat rice production statistics
nodes.title: BPS Jawa Barat rice production statistics
nodes.role: Provides provincial and regency rice-production quantities for supply-side modeling.

nodes.label: Rice field map and administrative boundaries
nodes.title: Rice field map and administrative boundaries
nodes.role: Provides spatial boundary and rice-field context for production and district geography.

nodes.label: OpenStreetMap West Java road network
nodes.title: OpenStreetMap West Java road network
nodes.role: Defines the routable network used for assigning modeled rice flows to road segments.

nodes.label: Hazard, market and rice-milling-unit sources
nodes.title: Hazard, market and rice-milling-unit sources
nodes.role: Open Data Jawa Barat and Dinas Pertanian Jabar provide hazard, market, and RMU context for prioritization.

nodes.label: A family of spatial interaction models
nodes.title: A family of spatial interaction models
nodes.role: Supports the gravity-model and spatial-interaction logic used to construct OD flows.

nodes.label: Doubly constrained OD matrix with Furness balancing
nodes.title: Doubly constrained OD matrix with Furness balancing
nodes.role: Balances production, processing-channel, and destination demand constraints before road assignment.

nodes.label: OSMnx, NetworkX, GeoPandas and Python workflow
nodes.title: OSMnx, NetworkX, GeoPandas and Python workflow
nodes.role: Implements network extraction, shortest paths, spatial joins, and browser-ready GeoJSON outputs.

nodes.label: TomTom POI and Routing API
nodes.title: TomTom POI and Routing API
nodes.role: Provides truck-profile routing and traffic context for checking modeled road priorities.

nodes.label: Agreement, sensitivity and Monte Carlo stable core
nodes.title: Agreement, sensitivity and Monte Carlo stable core
nodes.role: Tests whether the priority-road ranking survives alternate model assumptions and parameter variation.

nodes.label: Pantura priority-corridor finding
nodes.title: Pantura priority-corridor finding
nodes.role: Connects the modeled flows to a transport-planning priority for West Java food-security resilience.

mbr.title: Satellite Proxy for Low-Income Communities in DKI Jakarta
mbr.summary: This visual map separates satellite data, machine-learning methods, official validation sources, and interpretation cautions. The full reference list is preserved below.

center.label: Satellite proxy for low-income communities in DKI Jakarta
center.title: Can public satellite embeddings approximate the spatial pattern of low-income community morphology in Jakarta?

nodes.label: AlphaEarth satellite embeddings
nodes.title: AlphaEarth satellite embeddings
nodes.role: Provides the satellite embedding field used as the core spatial feature set.

nodes.label: Temporally harmonised gridded building-stock dataset
nodes.title: Temporally harmonised gridded building-stock dataset
nodes.role: Supports temporal building morphology context for interpreting urban form change.

nodes.label: BPS DKI Jakarta IPKS 2020
nodes.title: BPS DKI Jakarta IPKS 2020
nodes.role: Provides official social vulnerability dimensions used for convergent validation.

nodes.label: Meta Relative Wealth Index, Indonesia
nodes.title: Meta Relative Wealth Index, Indonesia
nodes.role: Provides an external wealth-index benchmark for interpreting the proxy.

nodes.label: Microestimates of wealth for LMICs
nodes.title: Microestimates of wealth for LMICs
nodes.role: Supports the broader premise of estimating wealth/deprivation with nontraditional spatial data.

nodes.label: Strengths and limitations of big-data relative wealth indices in Indonesia
nodes.title: Strengths and limitations of big-data relative wealth indices in Indonesia
nodes.role: Keeps limitations visible when interpreting proxy outputs in Indonesia.

nodes.label: XGBoost, temporal smoothing and quintile classification
nodes.title: XGBoost, temporal smoothing and quintile classification
nodes.role: Implements the two-stage proxy model, temporal artifact correction, and final classifications.

nodes.label: Statistical Power Analysis for the Behavioral Sciences
nodes.title: Statistical Power Analysis for the Behavioral Sciences
nodes.role: Supports effect-size interpretation in the validation table.

nodes.label: Spatial accessibility and transport inequity in Finland
nodes.title: Spatial accessibility and transport inequity in Finland
nodes.role: Provides a planning-practice reference for adapting equity analysis to a Jakarta poverty-mapping context.

nodes.label: Persistent deprived and worsening-neighborhood classifications
nodes.title: Persistent deprived and worsening-neighborhood classifications
nodes.role: Connects satellite morphology, smoothing, and validation to the final neighborhood trajectory categories.

cbd.title: Mapping Indonesia's Business Centers with DBSCAN
cbd.summary: This visual map shows how OSM data, DBSCAN theory, Python tooling, and completeness limitations support the CBD clustering story. The full reference list is preserved below.

center.label: Mapping Indonesia's business centers using DBSCAN
center.title: Where do commercial POIs form the main business center in each Indonesian province?

nodes.label: OpenStreetMap Indonesia commercial POIs
nodes.title: OpenStreetMap Indonesia commercial POIs
nodes.role: Provides the national POI substrate used to detect commercial clusters.

nodes.label: Provincial boundaries and province assignment
nodes.title: Provincial boundaries and province assignment
nodes.role: Constrains clustering and rankings to comparable province-level geographies.

nodes.label: Jakarta deep-dive POI subset
nodes.title: Jakarta deep-dive POI subset
nodes.role: Uses the separate Jakarta run of 15,417 POIs and 19 clusters to compare the national CBD ranking with a city-scale pattern.

nodes.label: A density-based algorithm for discovering clusters in large spatial databases with noise
nodes.title: A density-based algorithm for discovering clusters in large spatial databases with noise
nodes.role: Provides the DBSCAN clustering foundation for CBD detection.

nodes.label: Haversine metric and adaptive DBSCAN parameters
nodes.title: Haversine metric and adaptive DBSCAN parameters
nodes.role: Adapts density clustering to geographic coordinates and province-level variation.

nodes.label: scikit-learn DBSCAN and pyosmium PBF parsing
nodes.title: scikit-learn DBSCAN and pyosmium PBF parsing
nodes.role: Implements national POI extraction and clustering without paid APIs.

nodes.label: Python and Folium map outputs
nodes.title: Python and Folium map outputs
nodes.role: Packages the Jakarta and national CBD outputs into interactive maps embedded on the page.

nodes.label: OpenStreetMap completeness limitation
nodes.title: OpenStreetMap completeness limitation
nodes.role: Keeps the crowdsourced-data limitation visible when comparing regions.

nodes.label: Noise ratio and no-CBD diagnostic
nodes.title: Noise ratio and no-CBD diagnostic
nodes.role: Uses DBSCAN noise share and the 37/38 province result to keep sparse coverage and dispersed commercial form visible in the interpretation.

nodes.label: Downloadable CBD analysis JSON outputs
nodes.title: Downloadable CBD analysis JSON outputs
nodes.role: Publishes per-province CBD, top-cluster, and island-summary outputs for reuse.

nodes.label: CBD ranking and economic-geography interpretation
nodes.title: CBD ranking and economic-geography interpretation
nodes.role: Connects cluster size, density, tourism signals, and OSM coverage caveats to the final narrative.
